title: ECU Configuration Mismatch
description: The BCM reports that an ECU configuration does not match the expected Proxi configuration.
procedure: For B2204-55 Invalid/Incompatible Configuration, use the Proxi tool to check MCPA, MCPB, and SGCP alignment.
In BCM, read Proxi data using DID 22 20 23.
Verify the affected module is correctly EOL configured. Use the required diagnostic session, tester-present, and BCM security access before configuration changes.
For B2204-00 ECU Configuration Mismatch, perform manual Proxi alignment for the affected ECU.
BCM → PID Editor: read DID 22 20 23 and copy the response.
Remove the positive response prefix 62 20 23 from the copied response data.
In the affected ECU PID Editor, write the BCM configuration using: 2E 20 23 [configuration data].
Save the original BCM and ECU data before writing, and ensure vehicle power is stable.

title: Controlled System Shutdown
description: Active BPCM fault indicating a controlled system shutdown.
procedure: Enter an extended diagnostic session and complete the required BPCM security access.
Open Onboard Routines and search for “High impact...”.
In the second drop-down menu, select “Reset”, then select Play.
Search for “High isolation...”.
In the second drop-down menu, select “Reset”, then select Play.
Perform a soft reset of the BPCM.
Recheck the DTC status after the reset.

title: SGW in Plant Mode
description: Usually seen on LWB vehicles. The SGW may not have been flashed at the plant.
procedure: Verify the SGW software version.
In VSIR Loop 4.1, find and flash the PROD version of the SGW software.
Flash the PROD version even if the part number and software version appear identical to the version currently on the vehicle.
Confirm the SGW exits Plant Mode after flashing.

title: Ride Height Calibration Required
description: Ride-height calibration procedure for WS REPB/REEV vehicles only.
procedure: In ASCM, open On-board Routines.
Open “Leveling Set To” and select “Normal” ride height.
Open the Ride Height Calibration routine.
The correct method is to measure every corner and enter the measured values.
For WS REPB/REEV only, use rear target 51 mm for both sides and front target 114 mm for both sides.
If writing values fails, verify CDA is set to Metric. Change units through Edit → Settings, or convert the values to inches.
After the routine completes, exit Plant Mode using “Get Out of Plant Mode” → Run, then clear codes.

title: PAM in Plant Mode
procedure: Complete ADA Unlock for the PAM.
Enter an Extended Diagnostic Session with Tester Present.
Open the Plant Mode routine.
Select “Exit” from the right-side drop-down list.
Select the green Play button and confirm the routine completes successfully.
Clear the stored DTC.
Calibration-not-learned DTCs require calibration and may remain when vehicles are parked in Pilot.

title: Airbag Warning Lamp On After Proxi Alignment
description: Typically appears after performing Proxi Alignment.
procedure: In ORC, select Reset.
Perform a Hard Reset.
Clear codes.
Perform a key cycle.
Repeat reset, clear-code, and key-cycle steps if needed until the lamp clears.

title: Digital Crash Input Fault
description: Set in the BPCM but typically caused by the ORC detecting an incorrect crash input configuration.
procedure: Verify that the correct ORC EFD was used during flashing.
This fault has been associated with using an incorrect ORC EFD instead of the REPB version.
Reflash only step 5 using the correct REPB ORC Part Number/file.
Steps 1 through 4 do not need to be repeated.
Clear codes and confirm the fault does not return.

title: Placard Tire Pressure Fault
description: Placard tire-pressure procedure for WS REPB vehicles only.
procedure: In RFHUB, open Read/Write.
Filter for “Placard”.
Write both Front Placard and Rear Placard values.
Set Front Placard to 60 psi.
Set Rear Placard to 65 psi.
Convert to kPa if CDA is configured to use metric pressure units.

title: Selected Ride Height Not Permitted
description: Air suspension does not raise or lower, or the selected ride height is not permitted.
procedure: In BCM, open Proxi Data.
Filter for “Type” and find Drive Type.
Change Drive Type from “RWD” to “2-speed 4wd”.
Complete ADA Unlock and enter an Extended Diagnostic Session before writing.
Write the change; retry if the write fails.
Run the Proxi Alignment tool for the complete vehicle.
Clear codes and confirm air-suspension operation.
The suspension may not lower while in Park unless Entry/Exit mode is requested by double-tapping the down switch.

title: BSCM Configuration Mismatch / Proxi Alignment Failure
description: May include ABS and ESC warning lamps in the cluster.
procedure: In BCM, open Proxi Data.
Filter for “Steering”.
Find Steering Rack_Pinion Type and change the value to “Type 1”.
Complete ADA Unlock and enter an Extended Diagnostic Session before writing.
Write the change; retry if the write fails.
Run the Proxi Alignment tool for the complete vehicle.
Clear codes and check system function.
